item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's no hub. We have a draft but I don't think much movement has happened on it in years. Otherwise it meets the other requirements.

However, before the current requirements there was a significant amount of resistance to including on the GOI hub, for reasons I frankly don't understand. I wouldn't assume that it would be "official" even if it had a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the shark punching center count as an official GOI now? I noticed that this article is labeled as GOI format, but can't find much information about the SPC

item.title: Re: Wtf
item.content:encoded:
why is this

The short version is that I really liked MrWrong's SPC-2615, I wrote SPC-140 and then I thought about how the lizard would work in that world. Add in my thoughts about why the termination log is weird, and here we are.

item.title: Re: Wtf
item.content:encoded:
SPC is a site in-joke; newbies frequently misspell "SCP" as "SPC." This turned into snide remarks about the "Shark Punching Center" which eventually resulted in a couple joke SCPs. Then someone got the idea of "What if institutionalized shark-punching, but 4srs?"

This is a parody of SCP-682 done in the vein of that. Essentially, the SPC must punch sharks. Bad things happen if they do not. When presented with an immortal, invincible being, they offered it a job punching sharks. It refused. They found a way to turn it into a shark that can be punched forever.

item.title: Re: Wtf
item.content:encoded: Maybe read the author commentary on sirpudding's author page? http://www.scp-wiki.net/sirpudding-s-author-page

item.title: Wtf
item.content:encoded: Ok so just discovered this… and I’ll be honest I have no clue what I’m reading it. When first read the first paragraph I thought I was just being a moron, but then I read it again and realized ‘no I am just so fucking confused.’ What is this actually why is this? I’m so confused my head hurts, please someone anyone a mild clarifying thing please.

item.title: Math is broken. Send help.
item.content:encoded:
So I guess the page that this was referencing for the math bits went down. I don't know enough about the intertubes or CS to fix it on my own. I made a temporary fix so at least there's something there. I will try to get a hold of Crayne, in the meantime unless someone out there can help.

Incidentally, if this does get fixed I want to maybe change the formula for the first number as it hasn't quite been what I wanted it to be in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part has to be the fact that the second interview parodies the interview on the original page.

item.title: Wierd Math Finally Explained!
item.content:encoded: I've finally managed to post commentary for this article on my author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My work here is done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found the augmentation surgery section surprisingly horrifying.
It's not that unusual or excessive for the site, but some combination of the otherwise jokey nature of the page (and my expectations of what a SPC article would be), and the fact that 682, for better or worse, is a distinct character rather than a faceless number, cut straight through my usual clinical detachment and jumped straight to visceral visualisation.
Something about the pointlessness of it - the Foundation does infinitely horrible things for the protection of humanity. The SPC are torturing the lizard to… punch sharks?

tl;dr I was expecting a joke, I got body horror. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very close, yes. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here are my guesses. The first number has something to.do with the misspelling of SCP to SPC and connecting them to Swann's 001 proposal. The rest of the numbers have something to do with either how 682 is discussed in the forums or how 682 is perceived in-laws universe. All of this is done using regex or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's happening here? O.o

Right now, incorrect math. :(

Hopefully that first one will get fixed soon. When I do my author page commentary I will drop spoilers for these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit, this was hilarious. XD

I have to ask what's going on with that coding. I didn't even realize that was there until you mentioned it in the A/N. What's happening here? O.o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Punching Centre has more-or-less complete and total control over 682, something the Foundation never has. And they focus said control into punching sharks, or turning things into sharks so as to be punched.

My intent is that the Foundation can do this too (and effectively manipulate into it into a situation where successfully adapting to the environment requires helplessness) , but can't overcome the compulsion to try to kill it in order to ever figure this out. As it is, the acid bath is effective, but it keeps breaching because they keep letting it out in order to try to kill it.

I think we need to stop laughing at the SPC now, guys. They ain't funny no more.

My goal with these is dark humor and comedic horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Touchè

item.title: The future is now
item.content:encoded: Hᴀs sᴄɪᴇɴᴄᴇ ɢᴏɴᴇ ᴛᴏᴏ ꜰᴀʀ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually… kinda scary.

The Punching Centre has more-or-less complete and total control over 682, something the Foundation never has. And they focus said control into punching sharks, or turning things into sharks so as to be punched.

I think we need to stop laughing at the SPC now, guys. They ain't funny no more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: mother of god

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DAMN SHARKS ATTACKING MY FREAKING VIEW

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a goal, here, certainly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the closest I've come to feeling sorry for 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still funny and charming in execution so upvoted, but this whole "porting mainlist SCPs1 to SPCs2" thing is wearing thin. It was novel at first, but if this version of the SPC is going to be how their GoI Format works, I want to see some more original stuff.

Footnotes
1. I initially typo'd this as SPCs
2. Also initially typo'd this as SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview was amusing but the rest felt like a slog to read through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Per the new guidelines, we are only missing a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I didn't feel like "the lizard is totally cooperative with the Centre" is a) an interesting story and b) fits at all the nature of the lizard. The premise here is that the lizard is the same beast, but this world is crazier than it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can we please add the Shark Punching Center to the GOI Page now?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SPC-682 can become the ultimate weapon against sharks swimming in shallow waters around the coasts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has to do with MrWrong's central thesis about SPC-001

Note: We should, one day, collab on this. Two heads are better than one, as they say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pls keep the shark-punching selachian entity skips coming, I need them to live.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get the significance of the changing numbers

Hint: It has to do with MrWrong's central thesis about SPC-001 (also that first number isn't calculating correctly, I've PM'd Crayne since I don't actually have access to that end).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(including, but not especially, towards selachian entities)

That is perfect SPC right there.

I don't get the significance of the changing numbers (especially considering all the work required to put them there), but upvoted for a take on 682 that turns parody into sympathy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All things considered, I'm only glad that the technical issues have been resolved. And don't worry, this has its own good ring to the bitter end.

It's odd that the SPC's insanity has rendered a lot of things (including 682) relatively sane in comparison. Truly a mad world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. It's been turned into a shark. A shark that can be punched forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(How the hell did this joke get started)

Someone mistyped 'SCP' as 'SPC'.

When reviewing SPCs like this, should you treat them like -Js? If so +1.

I don't know. The concept is inherently absurd. I interpret it as a way to write about silly topics in the most serious way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now we wait for SPC-173

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, I laughed, therefore +1. Plus, the idea of forcing 682 into a certain way of being is fascinating to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the SPC out-crazying 682.
That last addendum is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There will be a day when punching sharks is not funny, but it is not this day.

I was wrong. -1. Might work as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(How the hell did this joke get started)
When reviewing SPCs like this, should you treat them like -Js? If so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's 682, but instead of a bad lizard it's the ultimate shark puncher.
+1

item.title: "Easily Punchable Shark" -- Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is cowritten by me and Crayne. I did the textual bits, he did the fairly extensive coding backend.

I apologize for the loading times, they weren't avoidable. I'll leave exactly what is being done in that time to the audience to try to figure out (and maybe drop some spoilers later).

Thanks to MrWrong, psul, and Petrograd for forum feedback. I'm afraid I ignored some of it this time, as I wasn't really able to make it work and retain my vision for the piece.

There are, I hope, some fairly cool world-building elements hidden in here, both for the SPC universe and in regards to SCP-682 itself.

I originally intended this, like my last poisson d’avril for April 1st, but it was delayed by the technical issues.

Commentary on my author page

Good punching, frogmen!
